tours.name: Trans Pamirs Tour: Osh → Dushanbe
tours.description: An unforgettable overland expedition across the legendary Pamir Highway from Osh to Dushanbe. Experience dramatic mountain landscapes, alpine lakes, remote villages, high-altitude passes, and authentic Pamiri culture along one of the world’s greatest road journeys.
tours.highlights: Osh City|Tulparkul Lake|Lenin Peak Base Camp|Kizilart Pass|Aqbaital Pass|Bulunkul Lake|Wakhan Valley|Yamchun Fortress|Bibi Fatima Hot Springs|Khorog Botanical Garden|Panj River|Hulbuk Palace|Nurek Dam

ru.name: Транспамирский тур: Ош → Душанбе
ru.description: Незабываемая наземная экспедиция по легендарному Памирскому тракту от Оша до Душанбе. Драматичные горные пейзажи, альпийские озёра, отдалённые деревни, высокогорные перевалы и подлинная памирская культура — одно из величайших дорожных путешествий в мире.

tg.name: Тури транспомирӣ: Ош → Душанбе
tg.description: Сафари беҳадуллоҳаи заминӣ дар Роҳи афсунии Помир аз Ош то Душанбе. Манзараҳои кӯҳӣ, кӯлҳои альпӣ, деҳаҳои дурдаст, гузаргоҳҳои баланд ва фарҳанги аслии помирӣ — яке аз бузургтарин сафарҳои роҳи ҷаҳон.

tours.name: Pamir Highway & Sarez Lake Adventure
tours.description: Embark on a 16-day immersive journey along Tajikistan’s legendary Pamir Highway across high-altitude deserts, ancient Silk Road settlements, and remote mountain villages — culminating in an unforgettable trek to the spectacular Sarez Lake.
tours.highlights: Pamir Highway|Sarez Lake Trek|Yamchun Fortress|Langar Petroglyphs|Vrang Stupa|Garm Chashma Hot Springs|Karakul Lake|Yashilkul Lake|Bartang Valley|Pamiri Villages

ru.name: Памирский тракт и озеро Сарез
ru.description: 16-дневное погружение в легендарный Памирский тракт Таджикистана: высокогорные пустыни, древние поселения Шёлкового пути и отдалённые горные деревни — с незабываемым треккингом к озеру Сарез.

tg.name: Роҳи Помир ва кӯли Сарез
tg.description: Сафари 16-рӯза дар Роҳи афсунии Помири Тоҷикистон: биёбонҳои баланд, ҷойҳои қадимии Роҳи Абрешим ва деҳаҳои кӯҳӣ — бо трекинги беҳадуллоҳа то кӯли Сарез.

tours.name: Fann Adventure: Lakes, Fortresses & Culture
tours.description: Explore Tajikistan’s diverse landscapes, ancient fortresses, Silk Road cities, alpine lakes, and mountain villages on a 15-day cultural and nature expedition through the Fann Mountains and beyond.
tours.highlights: Dushanbe|Hissor Fortress|Seven Lakes|Fan Mountains|Iskanderkul Lake|Panjakent|Khujand|Hulbuk Fortress|Karon City|Rasht Valley

ru.name: Приключение в Фаннах: озёра, крепости и культура
ru.description: 15-дневная культурно-природная экспедиция по разнообразным ландшафтам Таджикистана: древние крепости, города Шёлкового пути, альпийские озёра и горные деревни в горах Фанн и за их пределами.

tg.name: Сафари Фон: кӯлҳо, қалъаҳо ва фарҳанг
tg.description: Экспедитсияи 15-рӯзаи фарҳангӣ ва табиӣ дар манзараҳои гуногуни Тоҷикистон: қалъаҳои қадим, шаҳрҳои Роҳи Абрешим, кӯлҳои альпӣ ва деҳаҳои кӯҳӣ дар кӯҳҳои Фон.

tours.name: Dushanbe Samarqand Tour 2026-2027
tours.description: If you are planning  10-days tour from Dushanbe to Samarqand, you are in for an exciting journey through Central Asia. This route will take you through two beautiful countries, Tajikistan and Uzbekistan, allowing you to explore their rich history, culture, and natural beauty. In this comprehensive guide, we will provide you with a detailed itinerary and highlight the must-visit attractions along the way

ru.name: Тур Душанбе — Самарканд 2026–2027
ru.description: 10-дневное путешествие из Душанбе в Самарканд через два прекрасных государства — Таджикистан и Узбекистан. Богатая история, культура и природная красота Центральной Азии.

tg.name: Тури Душанбе — Самарқанд 2026–2027
tg.description: Сафари 10-рӯза аз Душанбе то Самарқанд тавассути ду кишвари зебо — Тоҷикистон ва Ӯзбекистон. Таърих, фарҳанг ва зебоии табиати Осиёи Марказӣ.

tours.name: Jizew Trekking Tour 2026-2027
tours.description: During this tour you would get acquainted with the historical and archeological sites of the Pamirs. You would enjoy the beautiful lake scenery of the village Jizev as well as would drive along the river Panj that makes the boundary line between Tajikistan and Afghanistan, the photogenic places with shrines, signs as well as the beautiful mountain scenery of the Pamirs. And last but not the least you would observe the preserved culture, tradition and the life style of the Tajik and Pamirian people in the remote villages of the Pamirs in Wakhan and Bartang Valley. See more about the tour in the itinerary below:

ru.name: Треккинг-тур Джизев 2026–2027
ru.description: Знакомство с историческими и археологическими памятниками Памира, живописное озеро в деревне Джизев, дорога вдоль реки Пяндж на границе с Афганистаном и традиции таджиков и памирцев в отдалённых селениях Вахана и Бартанга.

tg.name: Тури трекингии Ҷизев 2026–2027
tg.description: Ошноӣ бо ёдгориҳои таърихӣ ва бостоншиносии Помир, манзараи зебои кӯли деҳаи Ҷизев, роҳ дар канори дарёи Панҷ ва анъанаҳои тоҷикон ва помириён дар деҳаҳои дурдасти Вахон ва Бартанг.

tours.name: Jeep Tour to Fann Mountains 2026-2027
tours.description: The Fann Mountains are a popular destination for trekking and hiking in Tajikistan. The mountain range is located in the western part of the country, near the border with Uzbekistan. The Fann Mountains offer a variety of landscapes, including alpine lakes, glaciers, and peaks over 5,000 meters high.

ru.name: Джип-тур в горы Фанн 2026–2027
ru.description: Горы Фанн — популярное направление для треккинга в Таджикистане. Альпийские озёра, ледники и вершины выше 5000 метров на границе с Узбекистаном.

tg.name: Тури ҷип ба кӯҳҳои Фон 2026–2027
tg.description: Кӯҳҳои Фон — самти маъмул барои трекинг дар Тоҷикистон. Кӯлҳои альпӣ, глетчерҳо ва қуллаҳои болои 5000 метр дар наздикии марзи Ӯзбекистон.

tours.name: Dushanbe City Tour 2026-2027
tours.description:
Dushanbe, the capital city of Tajikistan, is a vibrant and culturally rich destination that offers a variety of attractions for visitors to explore. A 2-day city tour in Dushanbe allows travelers to experience the highlights of the city, including its historical sites, cultural landmarks, and natural beauty. In this comprehensive guide, we will outline an itinerary for a 2-day Dushanbe city tour, highlighting the must-visit places and providing useful information to make the most of your visit.

We will visit, Somoni Square, Rudaki Park, Flag Park, Indendent square, new Parliament building, Megon Market, Mosque,

ru.name: Городской тур по Душанбе 2026–2027
ru.description: Двухдневный тур по столице Таджикистана: исторические памятники, культурные достопримечательности и природная красота. Площадь Сомони, парк Рудаки, парк Флага, базар Мегон и другие highlights.

tg.name: Тури шаҳрӣ дар Душанбе 2026–2027
tg.description: Тури дуруза дар пойтахти Тоҷикистон: ёдгориҳои таърихӣ, ҷойҳои фарҳангӣ ва зебоии табиӣ. Майдони Сомонӣ, боғи Рӯдакӣ, боғи Парчам, бозори Мегон ва дигар ҷойҳо.

tours.name: Tours to Khatlon and Sughd 2026-2027
tours.description: We will visit the most historical cultural place in south and north of Tajikisan.
tours.location: Dushanbe  Chilichorchama, Iskandarkul  Panjakent, Uzbek border

ru.name: Тур по Хатлону и Согду 2026–2027
ru.description: Посещение важнейших историко-культурных мест юга и севера Таджикистана.
ru.location: Душанбе — Чиличорчама, Искандеркуль — Пенджикент, граница с Узбекистаном

tg.name: Тур ба Хатлон ва Суғд 2026–2027
tg.description: Боздид аз муҳимтарин ҷойҳои таърихӣ ва фарҳангии ҷануб ва шимоли Тоҷикистон.
tg.location: Душанбе — Чиличорчама, Искандаркул — Панҷакент, марзи Ӯзбекистон

tours.name: Travel to Bartang and Wakhan corridor 2026-2027
tours.description: We will trekking in the most beautiful place and Bartang valley and Wakhan Corridor.
tours.location: Dushanbe  Bartang valley & Wakhan corridor Dushanbe

ru.name: Путешествие в Бартанг и коридор Вахан 2026–2027
ru.description: Треккинг в одних из самых красивых мест — долина Бартанг и коридор Вахан.
ru.location: Душанбе — долина Бартанг и коридор Вахан — Душанбе

tg.name: Сафар ба Бартанг ва дарҳи Вахон 2026–2027
tg.description: Трекинг дар зеботарин ҷойҳо — водии Бартанг ва дарҳи Вахон.
tg.location: Душанбе — водии Бартанг ва дарҳи Вахон — Душанбе

tours.name: Tours to Khatlon and Sughd 2026-2027
tours.description: We will visit the most historical cultural place in south and north of Tajikisan.
tours.location: Dushanbe  Chilichorchama, Iskandarkul  Panjakent, Uzbek border

ru.name: Тур по Хатлону и Согду 2026–2027
ru.description: Посещение важнейших историко-культурных мест юга и севера Таджикистана.
ru.location: Душанбе — Чиличорчама, Искандеркуль — Пенджикент, граница с Узбекистаном

tg.name: Тур ба Хатлон ва Суғд 2026–2027
tg.description: Боздид аз муҳимтарин ҷойҳои таърихӣ ва фарҳангии ҷануб ва шимоли Тоҷикистон.
tg.location: Душанбе — Чиличорчама, Искандаркул — Панҷакент, марзи Ӯзбекистон

hero.headline: Where the mountains speak
hero.subline: Curated expeditions into Tajikistan's most remote and sacred landscapes — for those who travel to be transformed.

about.headline: Born from a love of the mountains
about.body1: TajDiscovery was founded by mountaineers and cultural explorers who fell deeply in love with Tajikistan's untouched grandeur. We believe travel should be transformative — not just a visit, but an encounter that reshapes how you see the world.
about.body2: Every expedition we craft is rooted in deep local knowledge, responsible tourism, and genuine human connection. We work exclusively with local guides, families, and communities — ensuring your journey benefits the people and places you encounter.

hero.headline: Где говорят горы
hero.subline: Кураторские экспедиции в самые отдалённые и священные ландшафты Таджикистана — для тех, кто путешествует, чтобы преобразиться.

about.headline: Рождённые любовью к горам
about.body1: TajDiscovery основан альпинистами и культурными исследователями, влюблёнными в нетронутое величие Таджикистана. Мы верим, что путешествие должно преображать — не просто визит, а встреча, меняющая взгляд на мир.
about.body2: Каждая экспедиция основана на глубоких местных знаниях, ответственном туризме и подлинной человеческой связи. Мы работаем только с местными гидами, семьями и сообществами.

hero.headline: Дар ин ҷо кӯҳҳо сухан мегӯянд
hero.subline: Экспедитсияҳои интихобшуда ба манзараҳои дурдаст ва муқаддаси Тоҷикистон — барои касе, ки барои тағйир сафар мекунад.

about.headline: Аз ишқи кӯҳҳо таваллуд ёфта
about.body1: TajDiscovery аз ҷониби кӯҳпаймоён ва муҳаққиқони фарҳангӣ таъсис ёфта, ки ба вусъати беназири Тоҷикистон мафтун шудаанд. Мо бовар дорем, ки сафар бояд тағйирдиҳанда бошад.
about.body2: Ҳар як экспедитсия дар асоси дониши маҳаллӣ, туризми масъул ва пайванди инсонӣ бунёд шудааст. Мо танҳо бо роҳнамоён, оилаҳо ва ҷомеаҳои маҳаллӣ кор мекунем.

recentBookings.name: Sultan Guliev
recentBookings.message: Hi Mansur, Can you quote me this tour please

bookings.name: Sultan Guliev
bookings.message: Hi Mansur, Can you quote me this tour please

highlights.title: Explore Osh City and Suleiman Mountain
Visit Tulparkul Lake near Lenin Peak Base Camp
Cross Kizilart and Aqbaital high mountain passes
Discover remote Bulunkul Lake and Wakhan Valley
Visit Yamchun Fortress and Bibi Fatima Hot Springs
Travel along the Panj River with views of Afghanistan
Explore Khorog Botanical Garden and Regional Museum
Visit Hulbuk Palace and view Nurek Dam
Travel the legendary Pamir Highway
Trek to remote Sarez Lake
Visit ancient Silk Road sites and fortresses
Relax in natural hot springs at Garm Chashma
Stay in traditional yurts and Pamiri guesthouses
Explore alpine lakes Karakul and Yashilkul
Discover the dramatic Bartang Valley landscapes
Experience authentic Pamiri culture and hospitality
Explore Dushanbe and Hissor Fortress
Visit the magnificent Seven Lakes and Fan Mountains
Relax at the breathtaking Iskanderkul Lake
Discover the ancient Sogdian city of Panjakent
Experience the culture and history of Khujand
Visit Hulbuk Fortress and the ancient Karon City
Travel through scenic mountain passes and valleys
Experience local guesthouses and authentic Tajik hospitality

itineraryDays.title: Arrival in Osh → City Tour
itineraryDays.subtitle: Osh, Kyrgyzstan
itineraryDays.description: Arrival in Osh and start of the city tour. Visit Suleiman Mountain, the Museum, and the famous Osh Bazaar before overnight stay in the city hotel.

itineraryDays.title: Osh → Sarymogol → Tulparkul Lake
itineraryDays.subtitle: Lenin Peak Base Camp Area
itineraryDays.description: Drive to Sarymogol village and continue toward Tulparkul Lake near Lenin Peak Base Camp. Enjoy dramatic mountain scenery and overnight in a traditional yurt camp.

itineraryDays.title: Tulparkul Lake → Traveler’s Pass → Karakul Lake
itineraryDays.subtitle: Kyrgyzstan–Tajikistan Border Region
itineraryDays.description: Morning hike to Traveler’s Pass at 4130 meters. Cross the border into Tajikistan and continue driving toward Karakul Lake surrounded by spectacular high-altitude landscapes.

itineraryDays.title: Karakul Lake → Aqbaital Pass → Murghab
itineraryDays.subtitle: Eastern Pamirs
itineraryDays.description: Drive through the dramatic Aqbaital Pass, the highest point of the journey at 4655 meters above sea level, before reaching Murghab village and exploring the local area.

itineraryDays.title: Murghab → Bulunkul Lake
itineraryDays.subtitle: Bulunkul Region
itineraryDays.description: Drive to the remote Bulunkul Lake area, famous for its harsh climate and isolated beauty. Experience authentic village life surrounded by spectacular Pamir landscapes.

itineraryDays.title: Bulunkul → Yashilkul → Khargushi Pass → Langar
itineraryDays.subtitle: Wakhan Corridor
itineraryDays.description: Visit Yashilkul Lake and continue over the Khargushi Pass at 4344 meters. Trek to Panorama Ridge at 4800 meters before driving to Langar village in the Wakhan Valley.

itineraryDays.title: Langar → Wakhan Valley → Khorog
itineraryDays.subtitle: Wakhan Valley & Khorog
itineraryDays.description: Visit Yamchun Fortress and Bibi Fatima Hot Springs while traveling through the beautiful Wakhan Valley. Continue to Khorog and explore the Regional Museum and Botanical Garden.

itineraryDays.title: Khorog → Kalaikhum
itineraryDays.subtitle: Panj River Route
itineraryDays.description: Drive along the Panj River bordering Afghanistan with breathtaking views of the Hindu Kush mountains and remote riverside villages.

itineraryDays.title: Kalaikhum → Dushanbe
itineraryDays.subtitle: Southern Tajikistan
itineraryDays.description: Drive toward Dushanbe with visits to the historic Hulbuk Palace and scenic viewpoints toward the famous Nurek Dam, one of the tallest hydropower stations in the world.

itineraryDays.title: Departure from Dushanbe
itineraryDays.subtitle: Dushanbe, Tajikistan
itineraryDays.description: Transfer to the airport and departure flight from Dushanbe. End of the Trans Pamirs overland adventure.

itineraryDays.title: Arrival in Dushanbe
itineraryDays.subtitle: Dushanbe
itineraryDays.description: Arrival in Dushanbe and transfer to the hotel. Free time to relax and prepare for the Pamir expedition.

itineraryDays.title: Explore Dushanbe
itineraryDays.subtitle: Capital City Tour
itineraryDays.description: Discover Dushanbe including the Botanical Garden, Mehrgon Bazaar, museums, and city landmarks before the journey into the Pamirs.

itineraryDays.title: Dushanbe → Norak Reservoir → Qalai Khumb
itineraryDays.subtitle: Western Pamirs
itineraryDays.description: Drive east through mountain roads with scenic views over the Norak Reservoir before reaching Qalai Khumb along the Panj River.

itineraryDays.title: Qalai Khumb → Khorog
itineraryDays.subtitle: Panj River Route
itineraryDays.description: Continue driving along the Panj River bordering Afghanistan toward Khorog, the capital of the GBAO region.

itineraryDays.title: Explore Khorog
itineraryDays.subtitle: Khorog City
itineraryDays.description: Visit the Ismaili Center, Pamir Botanical Garden, and local cultural sites while enjoying the atmosphere of Khorog.

itineraryDays.title: Khorog → Jelondy Hot Springs → Bulunkul
itineraryDays.subtitle: Eastern Pamirs
itineraryDays.description: Drive through high mountain scenery with a stop at Jelondy Hot Springs before reaching remote Bulunkul village.

itineraryDays.title: Bulunkul → Langar Pass Trek
itineraryDays.subtitle: Sarez Trek Begins
itineraryDays.description: Start the trekking expedition toward Sarez Lake through mountain passes and remote valleys surrounded by dramatic Pamir landscapes.

itineraryDays.title: Langar Pass → Uch Kul
itineraryDays.subtitle: High Mountain Trek
itineraryDays.description: Continue trekking through alpine terrain toward Uch Kul while enjoying panoramic mountain scenery.

itineraryDays.title: Uch Kul → Ukhinch
itineraryDays.subtitle: Remote Pamir Valleys
itineraryDays.description: Trek deeper into the remote Pamir region crossing rivers, valleys, and isolated landscapes toward Ukhinch.

itineraryDays.title: Tent Camp
itineraryDays.subtitle: Central Pamirs
itineraryDays.description: Continue the scenic trekking route approaching the spectacular Sarez Lake area surrounded by towering mountains.

itineraryDays.title: Arrival at Sarez Lake
itineraryDays.subtitle: Lake Sarez
itineraryDays.description: Reach the legendary Sarez Lake and enjoy breathtaking views over one of Central Asia’s most isolated alpine lakes.

itineraryDays.title: Explore Sarez Lake
itineraryDays.subtitle: Sarez Lake Area
itineraryDays.description: Free day at Sarez Lake with opportunities for hiking, photography, relaxing by the lake, or optional boat rides.

itineraryDays.title: Sarez Lake → Bartang Valley
itineraryDays.subtitle: Bartang Region
itineraryDays.description: Begin the return journey through the dramatic Bartang Valley with scenic landscapes and remote mountain villages.

itineraryDays.title: Bartang Valley → Barchidif
itineraryDays.subtitle: Bartang Valley
itineraryDays.description: Travel through the beautiful Bartang Valley toward Barchidif while experiencing authentic Pamiri village life.

itineraryDays.title: Barchidif → Qalai Khumb
itineraryDays.subtitle: Western Pamirs
itineraryDays.description: Return drive toward Qalai Khumb following mountain roads and scenic river valleys.

itineraryDays.title: Qalai Khumb → Dushanbe → Departure
itineraryDays.subtitle: Final Return
itineraryDays.description: Drive back to Dushanbe for final sightseeing, airport transfer, and departure flight marking the end of the Pamir and Sarez Lake adventure.

itineraryDays.title: Arrival in Dushanbe → Hissor → Dushanbe
itineraryDays.subtitle: Dushanbe & Hissor
itineraryDays.description: Arrival in Dushanbe and transfer to the hotel. Visit the Museum of Antiquities before driving to Hissor district to explore the famous Hissor Fortress, nearby madrasahs, and mausoleum. Return to Dushanbe in the evening.

itineraryDays.title: Dushanbe → Bokhtar → Ajina Teppa
itineraryDays.subtitle: Southern Tajikistan
itineraryDays.description: Drive south to Bokhtar through picturesque valleys and hills. Visit the Ajina Teppa archaeological site famous for its Buddhist relics and historical significance.

itineraryDays.title: Dushanbe → Iskanderkul
itineraryDays.subtitle: Fann Mountains
itineraryDays.description: Drive north through mountain passes toward the iconic Iskanderkul Lake surrounded by spectacular alpine scenery. Relax near the lake and enjoy panoramic mountain views.

itineraryDays.title: Iskanderkul → Alaudin (Artuch)
itineraryDays.subtitle: Alaudin Lakes Region
itineraryDays.description: Travel east toward Alaudin Lake and the Artuch camp through narrow mountain passes. Arrival in the Alaudin Lakes area with opportunities for short hikes and scenic walks.

itineraryDays.title: Artuch → Seven Lakes → Panjakent
itineraryDays.subtitle: Seven Lakes Region
itineraryDays.description: Drive toward the famous Seven Lakes and visit the colorful Marguzor Lakes where each lake has its own unique appearance. Continue to Panjakent in the afternoon.

itineraryDays.title: Panjakent City Tour
itineraryDays.subtitle: Ancient Sogdian City
itineraryDays.description: Explore the ancient city of Panjakent including the old city ruins, the Rudaki Museum, and local bazaars rich with culture and history.

itineraryDays.title: Panjakent → Istaravshan
itineraryDays.subtitle: Northern Tajikistan
itineraryDays.description: Drive east to Istaravshan and visit important historical sites including Mug Teppe fortress and the Kok Gumbaz mosque.

itineraryDays.title: Istaravshan → Khujand
itineraryDays.subtitle: Khujand Region
itineraryDays.description: Travel to Khujand and visit the Timurmalik Fortress, the Syr Darya River, and the Khujand Museum while exploring Tajikistan’s second-largest city.

itineraryDays.title: Khujand City Tour
itineraryDays.subtitle: Khujand
itineraryDays.description: Explore Khujand’s culture and traditions, ancient artifacts related to Alexander the Great, and post-Soviet architecture and monuments.

itineraryDays.title: Khujand → Obburdon → Dushanbe
itineraryDays.subtitle: Mountain Passes
itineraryDays.description: Drive back to Dushanbe with a stop at Obburdon to visit the historical mausoleum while crossing scenic mountain passes.

itineraryDays.title: Dushanbe → Hulbuk → Kalaikhumb
itineraryDays.subtitle: Pamir Gateway
itineraryDays.description: Travel toward Kalaikhumb with a visit to Hulbuk Fortress and scenic views from the Shurobod Pass overlooking the Panj River and Pamir Mountains.

itineraryDays.title: Kalaikhumb → Karon City Tour
itineraryDays.subtitle: Kalaikhumb → Karon City Tour
itineraryDays.description: Visit the ancient Karon City remnants including palace ruins, terraces, gardens, and historical structures connected to ancient trade routes and gold mining history.

itineraryDays.title: Kalaikhumb → Khaburabad Pass → Gharm (Jafr Village)
itineraryDays.subtitle: Rasht Valley
itineraryDays.description: Drive through the spectacular Khaburabad Pass toward the beautiful Rasht Valley and visit the Kuhsori Ajam Museum in Jafr village.

itineraryDays.title: Gharm (Jafr Village) → Dushanbe
itineraryDays.subtitle: Rasht Valley Return
itineraryDays.description: Enjoy the morning scenery of the Rasht Valley before driving back to Dushanbe through mountain landscapes and local villages.

itineraryDays.title: Transfer to Airport → Flight Home
itineraryDays.subtitle: Departure
itineraryDays.description: Transfer to the airport for your international departure flight home.

itineraryDays.title: Day 1 Arrival to Dushanbe
itineraryDays.description: Meeting in Dushanbe. After breakfast start of program. SS in Dushanbe, Visit to Gurminj Museum of traditional musical instruments and Museum of Antiquities and Hissar fortress with complex of museum and medresse, turn back to hotel, dinner in restaurant.

itineraryDays.title: Day 2 Dushanbe-Kurgantube
itineraryDays.description: After breakfast drive to Dusty square and visit monument of I. Somony.Then drive to KurganTube. Lunch in Kurgantube and then drive to Ajinateppa. Accommodation dinner and rest.

itineraryDays.title: Day 1: Rushon– Jizew
itineraryDays.description: Driving with car into the Bartang valley till the crossroad with the Jizew footpath. Two or three hours walk up to the pristine Pamiri village of Jizev, which has no road connection and is therefore still very traditional. It is situated in a valley with many small forests and marvellous milky-turquoise lakes. Time spent in car: approximately 6-7 hours. Walking time: 2-3 hours. Accommodation in homestay with nice manja (outdoor-bed)

itineraryDays.title: Day 2: Strolling around in Jizew valley
itineraryDays.description: Strolling around in Jizew valley. Walking up Jizew valley to the upper lakes and the pastures of the village, going as far as you wish. Walking time: As long as you want. Accomodation: homestay

itineraryDays.title: Day 3: Jizew – Khorog
itineraryDays.description: Jizew – Khorog, on foot and in jeep. Back down the way to Jizew Bridge on foot. By car drive to Khorog. Overnight in Khorog.

itineraryDays.title: Day 4: Khorog - Ishkashim
itineraryDays.description: Not far from Khorog (45 km) there is the famous Mineral Hot Spring-Garm Chashma and Ruby Mines of Khu i Lal (where Badakhshani rubies were mined as early as 14th century). The road to Ishkashim is smooth journey. Ishkashim is a small town at the mouth of the Wakhan corridor, where the 19th century Great Game ended. Continuing up northwards we will see amazing views of the Pamirs to the left and the Hindu Kush to the right; a number of peaks in view are above 6,000m. If we are lucky and we arrive to Ishkashim on Saturday, we will be able to visit the Crosse border Tajik-Afghan market on the Afghan side. There is no need to obtain Afghan visa for this visit. Guesthouse. B/L/D.

itineraryDays.title: Day 5: Ishkashim to Langar
itineraryDays.description: Ishkashim to Langar. On the way we will visit the Kah-Kaha in Namadgut one of the oldest fortress on the Silk Road, museum a local scholar, theologian, poet and traveler Sufi Muborak-Kadami Vakhani, who died in 1910. There is a stone pillar with a hole near his house, which he used to calculate a solar calendar. His house is decorated with inscriptions and decorations made by the scholar. On the way we will visit Buddhist Stupa in Vrang, sacred hot springs of Bibi Fatima Zahra, ruins of Yamchun fortress (IV-III B.C.) that once guarded Pamir branch of the Great Silk Road. Home stay. B/L/D.

itineraryDays.title: Day 6: Langar – Bulunkul and Yashil Kul Lake
itineraryDays.description: Langar – Bulunkul and Yashil Kul Lake: After breakfast we will leave the spectacular views of Afghanistan, with the ice-capped summit of the Wakhan Range, and the Pamir River and will cross Khargushi pass. We will arrive to Bulun Kul and Yashil Kul Lakes, where we will stay in a small hamlet of Bulunkul. The people living here are Pamiris, Tajiks and Kyrgyz nomads living in a harsh but hospitable environment. Here we will deepen our understanding of how people have adapted to a life in such remote mountainous areas. We will walk around the ruins of a caravanserai and solar calendar on the north side of Lake Yashil Kul. Homestay. B/L/D.

itineraryDays.title: Day 1: Arrival in Dushanbe
itineraryDays.description: Arrival in Dushanbe, the capital city of Tajikistan. Transfer to a hotel for overnight stay.

itineraryDays.title: Day 2: Drive from Dushanbe to Iskanderkul Lake
itineraryDays.description: Drive from Dushanbe to Iskanderkul Lake, a scenic alpine lake surrounded by mountains. Overnight stay in a guesthouse or yurt camp.

itineraryDays.title: Day 3: Trek from Iskanderkul Lake to Alauddin Lakes
itineraryDays.description: Trek from Iskanderkul Lake to Alauddin Lakes, passing through mountain passes and valleys. Overnight stay in a tent camp near the lakes.

itineraryDays.title: Day 4: Trek from Alauddin Lakes to Mutnoe Lake
itineraryDays.description: Trek from Alauddin Lakes to Mutnoe Lake, another picturesque alpine lake. Overnight stay in a tent camp near the lake.

itineraryDays.title: Day 5: Trek from Mutnoe Lake to Kulikalon Lakes
itineraryDays.description: Trek from Mutnoe Lake to Kulikalon Lakes, a group of three lakes at different altitudes. Overnight stay in a tent camp near the lakes.

itineraryDays.title: Day 6: Trek from Kulikalon Lakes to Artuch Alp Camp
itineraryDays.description: Trek from Kulikalon Lakes to Artuch Alp Camp, a base camp for climbing peaks in the area. Overnight stay in a tent camp or guesthouse.

itineraryDays.title: Day 7: Return to Dushanbe
itineraryDays.description: Return to Dushanbe. Departure from Dushanbe.

itineraryDays.title: Day 1: On the first day of your Dushanbe city tour
itineraryDays.description: On the first day of your Dushanbe city tour, you can start by visiting the National Museum of Tajikistan. Located in the heart of the city, this museum showcases the rich history and culture of Tajikistan through its extensive collection of artifacts and exhibits. The museum provides insights into the country's ancient civilizations, traditional arts and crafts, as well as its modern development. After exploring the museum, you can head to Rudaki Park, named after the famous Persian poet Rudaki. This park is a popular gathering spot for locals and offers a peaceful atmosphere with beautiful gardens and fountains. Take a leisurely stroll or find a cozy spot to relax and enjoy the surroundings. Next, make your way to Ismaili Center, an architectural masterpiece that serves as a cultural and religious center for the Ismaili community. The center features stunning design elements and offers guided tours that provide insights into Ismaili traditions and values. For lunch, you can try some local Tajik cuisine at one of the traditional restaurants in Dushanbe. Tajik food is known for its hearty flavors and includes dishes like plov (rice pilaf), shashlik (grilled meat skewers), and mantu (steamed dumplings). In the afternoon, visit Hissar Fortress, located just outside Dushanbe. This historical site dates back to the 18th century and offers a glimpse into the region's past. Explore the fortress walls, visit the museum inside, and enjoy panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. To end your first day in Dushanbe, head to Victory Park, a memorial complex dedicated to the memory of those who fought in World War II. The park features a towering monument, an eternal flame, and beautifully landscaped gardens. Take a moment to reflect and enjoy the peaceful ambiance of this important landmark.

itineraryDays.title: Day 2: On the second day of your Dushanbe city tour
itineraryDays.description: On the second day of your Dushanbe city tour, start by visiting the Somoni Monument, located in the heart of the city. This monument is dedicated to Ismail Samani, the founder of the Samanid dynasty and an important figure in Tajik history. The monument is an impressive architectural structure and serves as a symbol of national pride. Next, explore the Gurminj Museum of Musical Instruments, which showcases a diverse collection of traditional musical instruments from Tajikistan and Central Asia. Learn about the rich musical heritage of the region and enjoy live performances by local musicians. Afterward, make your way to the Botanical Garden of Dushanbe, a peaceful oasis in the city. This expansive garden is home to a wide variety of plant species from Tajikistan and other parts of the world. Take a leisurely walk through its lush greenery, admire colorful flowers, and relax in its tranquil atmosphere. For lunch, you can try some more local delicacies at one of Dushanbe's bustling bazaars or food markets. These markets offer a wide range of fresh produce, spices, and traditional snacks that will tantalize your taste buds. In the afternoon, visit Navruz Palace, an architectural gem that serves as a venue for cultural events and official ceremonies. Admire its grandeur from the outside or check if any events or exhibitions are taking place inside during your visit. To conclude your 2-day city tour in Dushanbe, head to the Central Park of Culture and Recreation. This park is a popular gathering spot for locals and offers various entertainment options, including amusement rides, cafes, and walking paths. Enjoy the lively atmosphere and take in the vibrant energy of the city. In summary, a 2-day city tour in Dushanbe allows visitors to explore the rich history, culture, and natural beauty of Tajikistan's capital. From historical sites like Hissar Fortress to cultural landmarks like Ismaili Center and the National Museum of Tajikistan, there is something for everyone to enjoy in this vibrant city.

itineraryDays.title: Day 01 Dushanbe sightseeing
itineraryDays.description: Meeting in Dushanbe international airport transfer to hotel. After breakfast start of program. Sightseeing in Dushanbe, Visit to Gurminj Museum of traditional musical instruments and Museum of Antiquities and Hissor fortress with complex of museum and medresse, coming back to hotel, dinner in the national restaurant Rokhat and relaxing.

itineraryDays.title: Days 02 Dushanbe-Kurgantube
itineraryDays.description: We will have breakfast and leaving Dushanbe for Kurgantube. Exploring Kurgantuber and having lunch. We will visit Ajinateppa and museum local market Ismoili Somoni overnight at hotel

itineraryDays.title: Days 03 Kurgantukbe-Shartuz-Bishkent-Shahrituz
itineraryDays.description: we will drive from Kurgantube to Shartuz and visiting Tahti Sangin. We will have lunch and visiting Takhti Sangin and then driving to Chilichorchashma in Bishkent and going back to Shahrituz overnight

itineraryDays.title: Days 04 Shahrituz-Kurgantube- Nurek
itineraryDays.description: we drive to Norak via Kurgantube Yavon. Visiting Nurek Dam which was built in 1980 on the Vakhsh River 75 km east of Tajikistan's capital Dushanbe. It is currently the tallest dam in the world with highest point located 310 m above its basement. Enjoying the beauty of the lake. Wondering around. Having lunch by the lake. Resting. Overnight in homestay.

itineraryDays.title: Days 05 Nurek-Iskanderkul
itineraryDays.description: We will drive from Nurek to Dushanbe and then to Iskandarkul Lake. Along the way, we will pass through Varzob Gorge, and over the Anzob Pass (3,373 m). Iskandarkul Lake is named after Alexander the Great and is one of the most beautiful mountain lakes in Central Asia. It is surrounded by the peaks of Chimtarga, Hazormech, Sarima, and Gazni. Alexander's armies passed this way on their route to conquest. There will be time for a short walk to see a 30m-high waterfall, snake lake and cave. Your accommodations will be in a clean, comfortable cottage.

itineraryDays.title: Days 06 Iskanderkul-Artuch
itineraryDays.description: After good relaxation we will leave Iskanderkul for Artuch. Artuch one of most beautiful campsite place for young tourists. We will hike a bit to chuqurak place to the top of mountain and coming back. overnight in Guest house

itineraryDays.title: Days 07 Artuch-Hafltkul (7 lake)
itineraryDays.description: We will explore anther direction of Artuch and driving to Haftkul. First will visit first lake then second lake, then third lake, then fourth lake, then fifth lake having lunch on six lake driving to the bottom of seven lake and hiking about 3 km to seven lake after exploring the lake coming back to six lake staying overnight.

itineraryDays.title: Days 08 Haftkul- Panjakent
itineraryDays.description: We will drive back to Panjakent taking photo passing by goldent mountain and having lunch in Panjakent and you will have free time to make friend with local people.

itineraryDays.title: Days 09 Sightseeing in Panjakent
itineraryDays.description: We will discover all part of Panjakent like museum, local market and other historical place. Panjakent was re-discovered in the mid-twentieth century and is still an active archaeological site. Visit the excavation site of the ancient Sogdian city from the 5th to 7th centuries. Sogdians were important traders during the that day of the Silk Road trade routes and are thought to have been Iranian in origin. Panjakent is often referred to as the "Pompeii of Central Asia." Visit the Panjakent Museum. Then continue to the ancient settlement of Sarazm, which is said to have existed 3400- 2000 B.C. Numerous homes, religious buildings, and palaces have been excavated in Sarazm. Many articles made of copper, bronze, lead, silver, and gold, as well as weaponry and ornaments of the 4th to 2nd millennium B.C., have been found at the site. You will be accommodated at a modern and comfortable hotel.

itineraryDays.title: Days 10 Panjakent-Samarqand
itineraryDays.description: After breakfast at 8-00 AM we will visit Sarazm and having, lunch and then drive to Tajik/Uzbek border and crossing through border and then drive to Samarkand.

itineraryDays.title: Day 01 Meeting at airport, transfer to hotel and sighseeing
itineraryDays.description: Our guide will meet you at the airport and accompany to the hotel. In the morning we will drive 30 km to visit Hissar fortress (VII- XVII centuries). This historical site has fortified areas and citadel. The fortress used to be the residence of the delegate of the Emir of Bukhara; it was located on a high hill and was well defended with 1 m thick walls and loopholes for guns and cannons. Once we are back to Dushanbe, first of all we will visit Rudaki Park, National library, White House, National state seal, House of parliament and, Somoni monument. After it we will stroll in National park to make photos of Ministry of Inner Affairs and the second biggest National Flag in the world – 165 m high pole, 30 m wide, 60 m long. After excursion in Central Museum you will have lunch. Then you will visit to Gurminj Museum of traditional musical instruments. To feel real life you should visit Asian bazaar. It has colorful goods, fruits and vegetables. We will see today the main highlights of Dushanbe and you will find out many interesting facts about Tajik people. Dinner will be in a traditional restaurant where you try Tajik food. Night is in a hotel.

itineraryDays.title: Days 02 Dushanbe-Nurek-Kalai Khumb 370 km (7-8 hours drive)
itineraryDays.description: Today we will drive to Kalai Khum village. You will see Nurek water reservoir that is called Tajik Sea. The highest point of the day is Shurabad pass (2 267 m). We will then drive along Afghanistan border. This part will amaze you by its unique mountains – it is already Territory of Pamir Mountains. Over the centuries, the Pamirs have inspired the world’s greatest explorers – Marco Polo, Hsuan Tsang, Mirza Muhammad Haidar. And now you also have a chance to travel here. You will drive along natural border – Panj River. Having visited the Pamirs, many travellers called this giant mountain system the ocean of white and blue mountains. This night you will spend in a home stay to encounter how local people live here.

itineraryDays.title: Days 03 Kalai Khumb-Ems 185 km ( 4.5-5 hours
itineraryDays.description: In the morning we drive to Rushan. 10 km from Kalai – Khum we will hike up to the ancient Old city of Karon. This old city was built in 3 century and in 2013 was restored. Ruins of the ancient settlement of Karon lie not far away from the village Ruzvai placed on the mountain Zogic at an altitude of 1700m. Zoroastrian sacred river Vanaguh Doytijo bends around the mountain from the east south and west. There is the tower of sacred fire built on a cruciform platform in pre-Islamic architecture style, which dates back to the ancient period of history. Then you will drive to Ems village (Rushan). Overnight and dinner is in a home stay.

itineraryDays.title: Days 04 Ems-Bardara 72 km ( 4.5 hours on offroad )
itineraryDays.description: Today you will drive to Bartang valley stopping in each village making friends, buying some handicraf and getting to start of trekking point Bardara. overnight at guest house

itineraryDays.title: Days 05 Bardara-Yelga ( 2900-3600 m)
itineraryDays.description: In the morning we will meet with the crew and get ready as we fix the luggage on donkeys. Our hiking starts from Bardara towards Bardara valley. The trail is quite easy and some parts of it are stretched along the Bardara river, so you can enjoy freshness and breeze coming from it. Camping is near Yelga where people in the past used to cultivate wheat for their livestock. Overnight in tents (3600 m). Hiking net 4 hours.

itineraryDays.title: Days 06 Yelga-below Lazar pass (3600 – 3700 – 4000 m)
itineraryDays.description: In the morning we start trekking towards the pass along Bardara valley until Gulzor. After a lunch break, we will continue trekking until we reach a camping point just below Shtik Lazar pass (4800m). Overnight in tents (4000 m). Hiking net 4/5 hours

itineraryDays.title: Days 07 Below the pass-Zaroj lek-Khabur (4000 – 4800 – 4518 – 4430)ga
itineraryDays.description: We start early in the morning before sunrise. Our plan is to cross the Shtik Lazar pass and walk about 5 km through the snow. If donkeys struggle to pass the snow the crew will help us to carry the luggage as porters. On the other side of the pass a new crew from Bachor will pick us up and accompany us. We will have a picnic near Kulen Lake. After the picnic, we will go for a short sightseeing trek towards Zaroj (4518 m) which lasts around 2.5 hours. Once we are back to the lake we head to Khaburga and camp there (4430 m). Hiking net 7/8 hours.

itineraryDays.title: Days 08 Khaburga-Vikhinch-Chapdara lake (4340 – 4100 – 4200 – 4553 m)
itineraryDays.description: In the morning we start trekking along Vikhinj valley. On the way, we will see many beautiful lakes and panoramas. We will stop for a lunch break near Uchkul lakes (Three lakes) where you can relax and catch fish. Later we will continue trekking up until Chapdar Lake (4553 m). This is the highest lake in Central Asia. Overnight in tents on a lake shore (4553 m). Hiking net 6 hours.

itineraryDays.title: Days 09 Chapdara-Kyrgyzshabar (4553 – 4200 m)
itineraryDays.description: In the morning we going down along Chapdar valley. According to your wish, you can take another route via Langar pass, so it will be 5km extra. Camping is in Kyrgyz-shabar. 19km – 7-8 hours walk. Overnight in tents at 4200 m.

itineraryDays.title: Days 10 Kyrgyzshabar- Langarghor-west (4200 – 3720 m) end of Yashikul
itineraryDays.description: In the morning we gradually hike down towards Langar gate. The decent is quite easy. Lunch break at Langar gate. We will then cross Chashma river and continue hiking towards Langarghev until we arrive at the West end of Yashilkul lake. Overnight in tents (3720 m). Hiking net 6/7 hours

itineraryDays.title: Days 11 West end of Yashikul-Bulunkul (25 km, 1 hour
itineraryDays.description: The most beautiful lakes of the Pamirs are Yashil Kul and Bulunkul (Blue lakes) stretched on the altitude of 3700 meters above sea level. Today we will spend a relaxing day near them. The color of the water in Bulunkul is a subtle interplay of violet and dark-blue in the sunlight. Species of fish like are found in abundance in the lake. There is the opportunity to see yaks, a unique species of domestic animals, sharing features of cow and sheep, traditionally used as a beast of burden apart from giving milk and meat products. You will drive to visit the hot spring and geyser near the lake of Bulunkul. Dinner and night is in a home stay.

itineraryDays.title: Days 12 Bulunkul-Langar (145 km, 5-6 hours)
itineraryDays.description: In the morning we will drive to Langar. On the way, you will drive over Khargush pass (4 344 m). The almost inaccessible landscape and rocky mountains of this corner of the world have given safe haven to a unique diversity of wildlife. Amongst the most common species of wild mammals are Turkestan rat, tiger-cat, grizzly bear, badger, stoat, fox, gray and red wolves. Pamirs provides a large and thriving population of Marco Polo sheep, the largest sheep in the world, with all year round and a strong population of the highly endangered snow leopard. You will climb up above Langar village to see petroglyphs. Dinner and night is in a home stay.

itineraryDays.title: Days 13 Langar-Ishkashim (140 km, 5 hours)
itineraryDays.description: Drive towards the Wakhan corridor. There are several places of interest to stop. Above the village of Vrang, on a cliff-side pitted with caves, stands a Buddhist complex dating from 4th- 7th centuries. At Yamg, we visit the reconstructed home of Sufi mystic Muabara Kadam , who died in 1910. Near this house there is a stone pillar with a hole in it which he used to calculate a solar calendar. On the road we will visit Kah-Kaa and Yamchun fortresses, further up the hillside are the hot spring at Bibi Fatima: dense and very salty minerals, the water at Bibi Fatima is crystal clear. Overnight and dinner is in guesthouse.

itineraryDays.title: Days 14 Ishkashim -Khorog (110 km, 4-4.5 hours)
itineraryDays.description: Today we will drive along Tajik-Afgan border to Khorog. It is perhaps one of the tiniest cities on the map of the world. It is surrounded by rocky mountains from all four sides. The town was built on the confluence of the 3 rivers where Shokhdara river and Ghund river flow into Panj river. On the way to Khorog you will meet Pamir people. A number of anthropological features set the Pamir people apart from the Tajik people. Among the Pamiris, there is a high percentage of people with blonde hair, fair skin and blue eyes. Pamirs Mountains are the highest mountains in the world with human population after the Himalayas in Tibet. Life in the mountains made traditions of local people different from Tajik people. On the way you will visit Garmchashma hot spring. overnight in Lal hotel

itineraryDays.title: Days 15 Khorog-Kalai Khumb (240 km, 7 hours)
itineraryDays.description: More than half of the road (from Rushan to Kalai Khum) is the same as you had before. This area consists of high rocky mountains over 6 000 meters and only 1 road was possible to be built. Passing through the Pamirs, the tourists have the rare opportunity to not only marvel the beauty of the virgin environment but also know more about the ethnography, history and everyday life of mountain dwellers and with this imagine how the history of humanity evolved as in the story of creation. On the way,we will stop in small villages to communicate with local people and to see their life. Human settlements are located on both sides of the river in narrow mountain valleys. On the other side of the river, you will see Afghan stone villages and gardens. Night is in a home stay.

itineraryDays.title: Days 16 Kalai Khumb-Dushanbe (370 km, 7-8 hours)
itineraryDays.description: In the morning we will drive to Dushanbe via Kulob. The road goes through the most picturesque river valley and amazing gorges in the terrain, where one can stop for rest and shoot photos of the mountain and rivers. On the way you will see small villages and meet local people. Tajiks are by nature hard working, hospitable, and kind-hearted. This nation greatly respects its traditions and folklore. This trip is attractive for the lovers of exotics, those curious about the ethnic and cultural diversity and captivated by the beauty of wild nature. Dinner will be in a restaurant. Night is in a hotel.

itineraryDays.title: Days 17 Depatures
itineraryDays.description: We trasfer you to airport saying goodbye to and wishing you safe landing

itineraryDays.title: Day 01 Dushanbe sightseeing
itineraryDays.description: Meeting in Dushanbe international airport transfer to hotel. After breakfast start of program. Sightseeing in Dushanbe, Visit to Gurminj Museum of traditional musical instruments and Museum of Antiquities and Hissor fortress with complex of museum and medresse, coming back to hotel, dinner in the national restaurant Rokhat and relaxing.

itineraryDays.title: Days 02 Dushanbe-Kurgantube
itineraryDays.description: We will have breakfast and leaving Dushanbe for Kurgantube. Exploring Kurgantuber and having lunch. We will visit Ajinateppa and museum local market Ismoili Somoni overnight at hotel

itineraryDays.title: Days 03 Kurgantukbe-Shartuz-Bishkent-Shahrituz
itineraryDays.description: We will drive from Kurgantube to Shartuz and visiting Tahti Sangin. We will have lunch and visiting Takhti Sangin and then driving to Chilichorchashma in Bishkent and going back to Shahrituz overnight

itineraryDays.title: Days 04 Shahrituz-Kurgantube- Nurek
itineraryDays.description: We drive to Norak via Kurgantube Yavon. Visiting Nurek Dam which was built in 1980 on the Vakhsh River 75 km east of Tajikistan's capital Dushanbe. It is currently the tallest dam in the world with highest point located 310 m above its basement. Enjoying the beauty of the lake. Wondering around. Having lunch by the lake. Resting. Overnight in homestay.

itineraryDays.title: Days 05 Nurek-Iskanderkul
itineraryDays.description: We will drive from Nurek to Dushanbe and then to Iskandarkul Lake. Along the way, we will pass through Varzob Gorge, and over the Anzob Pass (3,373 m). Iskandarkul Lake is named after Alexander the Great and is one of the most beautiful mountain lakes in Central Asia. It is surrounded by the peaks of Chimtarga, Hazormech, Sarima, and Gazni. Alexander's armies passed this way on their route to conquest. There will be time for a short walk to see a 30m-high waterfall, snake lake and cave. Your accommodations will be in a clean, comfortable cottage.

itineraryDays.title: Days 06 Iskanderkul-Artuch
itineraryDays.description: After good relaxation we will leave Iskanderkul for Artuch. Artuch one of most beautiful campsite place for young tourists. We will hike a bit to chuqurak place to the top of mountain and coming back. overnight in Guest house

itineraryDays.title: Days 07 Artuch-Hafltkul (7 lake)
itineraryDays.description: We will explore anther direction of Artuch and driving to Haftkul. First will visit first lake then second lake, then third lake, then fourth lake, then fifth lake having lunch on six lake driving to the bottom of seven lake and hiking about 3 km to seven lake after exploring the lake coming back to six lake staying overnight.

itineraryDays.title: Days 08 Haftkul- Panjakent
itineraryDays.description: We will drive back to Panjakent taking photo passing by goldent mountain and having lunch in Panjakent and you will have free time to make friend with local people.

itineraryDays.title: Days 09 Sightseeing in Panjakent
itineraryDays.description: We will discover all part of Panjakent like museum, local market and other historical place. Panjakent was re-discovered in the mid-twentieth century and is still an active archaeological site. Visit the excavation site of the ancient Sogdian city from the 5th to 7th centuries. Sogdians were important traders during the that day of the Silk Road trade routes and are thought to have been Iranian in origin. Panjakent is often referred to as the "Pompeii of Central Asia." Visit the Panjakent Museum. Then continue to the ancient settlement of Sarazm, which is said to have existed 3400- 2000 B.C. Numerous homes, religious buildings, and palaces have been excavated in Sarazm. Many articles made of copper, bronze, lead, silver, and gold, as well as weaponry and ornaments of the 4th to 2nd millennium B.C., have been found at the site. You will be accommodated at a modern and comfortable hotel.

itineraryDays.title: Days 10 Panjakent-Samarqand
itineraryDays.description: After breakfast at 8-00 AM we will visit Sarazm and having, lunch and then drive to Tajik/Uzbek border and crossing through border and then drive to Samarkand.

inclusions.text: Accommodation in hotels, guesthouses, and yurts
All border permits and local taxes
Wakhan Valley and Pamir Highway sightseeing program
GBAO, Sarez Lake and National Park permits
Accommodation in city hotels, guesthouses, traditional homestays and tents
Full board meals: breakfast, lunch and dinner
Donkeys with owner to carry luggage during the trek
All guided site visits and monument entrance fees
Local English-speaking guide and logistical support
Accommodation in hotels, guesthouses, and homestays
Breakfast, lunch, and dinner throughout the tour
Museum and monument entrance fees included in the itinerary
Visit to Hissor Fortress and Ajina Teppa archaeological site
Seven Lakes and Fan Mountains sightseeing program

faqs.question: Do I need a visa for Tajikistan and Kyrgyzstan?
faqs.answer: Most travelers require a Tajikistan e-visa and sometimes a GBAO permit for the Pamir region. Visa requirements for Kyrgyzstan depend on your nationality. We recommend checking current regulations before travel.

faqs.question: What is the highest altitude during the tour?
faqs.answer: The highest point of the expedition is Aqbaital Pass at approximately 4655 meters above sea level.

faqs.question: Is the tour physically demanding?
faqs.answer: The tour is considered moderate. Long driving days and high-altitude conditions require reasonable physical fitness, but no technical climbing experience is necessary.

faqs.question: What type of accommodation is provided?
faqs.answer: Accommodation includes hotels, guesthouses, homestays, and traditional yurt camps depending on the region visited.

faqs.question: Are meals included during the expedition?
faqs.answer: Breakfasts are included. Lunches and dinners are usually taken in local cafes or guesthouses and paid separately unless stated otherwise.

faqs.question: Is altitude sickness a concern?
faqs.answer: Yes, some areas of the Pamirs are above 4000 meters. We recommend proper acclimatization, hydration, and consulting your doctor before the trip.

faqs.question: What kind of vehicles are used?
faqs.answer: The expedition uses reliable 4x4 vehicles suitable for mountain roads and remote terrain conditions along the Pamir Highway.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary change because of weather or road conditions?
faqs.answer: Yes, mountain weather and road conditions can occasionally require route adjustments for safety reasons during the expedition.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Sarez Lake trek?
faqs.answer: The trek is considered challenging due to long hiking days, remote mountain terrain, and high-altitude conditions in the Pamirs.

faqs.question: What is included in the tour price?
faqs.answer: The tour includes accommodation, full board meals, permits, private transportation, 4x4 overland transport, guides, luggage donkeys during the trek, and sightseeing entrance fees.

faqs.question: Do I need a GBAO permit for this expedition?
faqs.answer: Yes. The GBAO permit, Sarez Lake permit, and National Park permits are included in the tour package.

faqs.question: What type of accommodation is provided?
faqs.answer: Accommodation includes city hotels, guesthouses, traditional Pamiri homestays, and tent camps during the trekking section.

faqs.question: Are meals included during the expedition?
faqs.answer: Yes. Full board meals including breakfast, lunch, dinner, and bottled water are included every day.

faqs.question: Will luggage be carried during the trek?
faqs.answer: Yes. Donkeys with local handlers are provided to transport luggage during the Sarez Lake trekking section.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights of the expedition?
faqs.answer: Highlights include the Pamir Highway, Sarez Lake trek, Silk Road sites, alpine lakes, Wakhan landscapes, hot springs, and authentic Pamiri hospitality.

faqs.question: What is the group size for this expedition?
faqs.answer: The expedition operates for groups from 1 to 15 participants.

faqs.question: What is the best season for this tour?
faqs.answer: The best seasons for this tour are spring, summer, and autumn when mountain roads and lake regions are most accessible.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights of the expedition?
faqs.answer: The tour includes visits to the Seven Lakes, Fan Mountains, Iskanderkul Lake, Panjakent, Khujand, Hulbuk Fortress, and the ancient Karon archaeological site.

faqs.question: What type of accommodation is provided?
faqs.answer: Accommodation includes hotels, guesthouses, and homestays throughout the expedition.

faqs.question: Are meals included during the tour?
faqs.answer: Yes. Breakfast, lunch, and dinner are included throughout the program.

faqs.question: Is the tour physically difficult?
faqs.answer: The tour is considered moderate and includes long scenic drives, light hiking opportunities, and travel through mountain regions.

faqs.question: Will we visit historical and cultural sites?
faqs.answer: Yes. The itinerary includes Hissor Fortress, Ajina Teppa, Mug Teppe Fortress, Kok Gumbaz Mosque, Hulbuk Fortress, and Karon old city.

faqs.question: What mountain regions are included in the route?
faqs.answer: The expedition passes through the Fan Mountains, Seven Lakes region, Shurobod Pass, and the scenic Rasht Valley.

faqs.question: Is airport transfer included?
faqs.answer: Yes. Arrival transfer in Dushanbe and departure airport transfer are included in the tour program.
